description: Competency taxonomy for engineering and technical management roles. Used by Growth Compass to tag skills and assess levels.

system_design.label: System Design
system_design.description: Ability to design scalable, reliable systems with appropriate tradeoffs

levels.aware: Understands basic architecture patterns. Can read and interpret existing system designs.
levels.practicing: Designs components within an existing architecture. Seeks review on larger decisions.
levels.capable: Designs systems independently. Makes explicit tradeoffs and documents rationale.
levels.proficient: Designs cross-system architectures. Anticipates failure modes and scaling bottlenecks.
levels.master: Sets architectural standards for the organization. Designs for 10x growth and organizational constraints.

levels.aware: Writes working code. Aware of style guides and testing concepts.
levels.practicing: Consistently writes tests. Code is readable and follows team conventions.
levels.capable: Designs APIs and abstractions well. Code is a model for peers. Catches issues in review.
levels.proficient: Raises the team's code quality bar. Introduces better patterns and tooling.
levels.master: Defines quality standards adopted across teams. Code influences practices beyond the organization.

debugging.label: Debugging & Problem Solving
debugging.description: Systematically diagnoses and resolves complex technical issues

levels.aware: Can follow a runbook. Knows how to search logs and ask for help.
levels.practicing: Debugs independently within owned services. Forms hypotheses before acting.
levels.capable: Debugs across system boundaries. Teaches debugging approach to others.
levels.proficient: Diagnoses novel problems with no prior art. Builds tools to make debugging faster for the team.
levels.master: Designs systems that are intentionally debuggable. Incident postmortems become industry references.

ai_building.label: AI Building
ai_building.description: Ability to build, deploy, and iterate on AI-powered solutions

levels.aware: Uses AI tools daily. Understands prompt engineering basics. Has built simple workflows.
levels.practicing: Builds AI agents and workflows for personal or team use. Writes structured prompts. Experiments with new AI tools.
levels.capable: Ships AI solutions that solve real business problems. Designs modular agent architectures. Understands evaluation and iteration.
levels.proficient: Builds AI systems used across teams. Combines knowledge bases, skills, and MCP. Establishes AI development standards.
levels.master: Creates novel AI architectures. Teaches AI building methodology. Defines what good AI engineering looks like for the organization.

data_thinking.label: Data Thinking
data_thinking.description: Ability to define metrics, analyze data, and make data-informed decisions

levels.aware: Can read basic dashboards and SQL queries.
levels.practicing: Writes queries to answer own questions. Defines metrics for owned features.
levels.capable: Designs measurement frameworks. Distinguishes correlation from causation. Communicates findings clearly.
levels.proficient: Identifies what should be measured when the domain is ambiguous. Builds data pipelines and self-serve analytics.
levels.master: Creates a data-informed culture. Defines organization-level metrics that drive good decisions.

user_insight.label: User Insight
user_insight.description: Understands user needs deeply, not just stated requirements

levels.aware: Reads user feedback. Can restate what users said.
levels.practicing: Conducts user interviews. Distinguishes what users say from what they need.
levels.capable: Identifies unmet needs. Prioritizes based on user impact, not just effort.
levels.proficient: Develops user empathy across the team. Decision-making consistently references user context.
levels.master: Sees user needs before users do. Creates products that change behavior.

problem_definition.label: Problem Definition
problem_definition.description: Structures fuzzy problems into clear, solvable definitions

levels.aware: Can work on well-defined problems. Needs clear requirements to start.
levels.practicing: Asks clarifying questions. Breaks down assigned problems into tasks.
levels.capable: Takes a vague need and defines the problem independently. Scopes appropriately.
levels.proficient: Frames problems the team didn't know it had. Reframes solutions into problem spaces.
levels.master: Defines the problem space for an entire domain. Others adopt the framework.

shipping.label: Shipping & Iteration
shipping.description: Delivers value in small, fast cycles and learns from each iteration

levels.aware: Completes assigned tasks. Follows the team's release process.
levels.practicing: Breaks work into shipable increments. Ships independently within team scope.
levels.capable: Ships MVPs to learn, not just to deliver. Iterates based on real feedback. Manages scope vs. time tradeoffs.
levels.proficient: Creates team velocity. Removes shipping friction. Decisions consistently optimize for learning speed.
levels.master: Defines shipping culture for the organization. Time-to-learning becomes a competitive advantage.

communication.label: Communication
communication.description: Communicates clearly and adapts to audience

levels.aware: Communicates within the team. Needs guidance on what to communicate to whom.
levels.practicing: Writes clear design docs and updates. Adapts detail level to audience.
levels.capable: Communicates complex ideas simply. Facilitates productive discussions. Gives and receives difficult feedback well.
levels.proficient: Influences without authority. Communicates vision that aligns teams. Handles external communication.
levels.master: Organizational communication is transformed. People reference communication artifacts as exemplars.

levels.aware: Shares knowledge when asked. Answers questions helpfully.
levels.practicing: Onboards new team members well. Provides timely, specific code review feedback.
levels.capable: Formally mentors junior engineers. Creates learning resources. Notices when someone needs support.
levels.proficient: Develops multiple people simultaneously. Creates growth opportunities for others. Team capability visibly grows.
levels.master: Builds a culture of development. Multiplies through coaches, not just direct mentoring.

initiative.label: Initiative & Ownership
initiative.description: Sees what needs to be done and drives it forward

levels.aware: Completes assigned work. Escalates blockers appropriately.
levels.practicing: Identifies problems in owned areas and drives them to resolution.
levels.capable: Takes on problems outside direct ownership. Drives cross-team initiatives. Persists through ambiguity.
levels.proficient: Creates initiatives that others rally around. Defines what the team should work on, not just how.
levels.master: Shapes organizational direction. Problems don't need owners because this person exists.

decision_making.label: Decision Making Under Uncertainty
decision_making.description: Makes sound decisions with incomplete information

levels.aware: Follows existing decision frameworks. Asks for guidance on ambiguous choices.
levels.practicing: Makes reversible decisions quickly. Documents assumptions. Knows when to escalate.
levels.capable: Makes tradeoffs explicit. Communicates decision rationale. Revisits decisions when assumptions change.
levels.proficient: Makes high-stakes decisions with incomplete data. Creates decision frameworks for others.
levels.master: Organization makes faster, better decisions because of frameworks this person established.

ai_native_mindset.label: AI-Native Mindset
ai_native_mindset.description: Thinks and operates with AI as a core capability, not an add-on

ai_first_thinking.label: AI-First Thinking
ai_first_thinking.description: Defaults to solving problems with AI, not adding AI to existing solutions

levels.aware: Uses AI chatbots to answer questions and draft content.
levels.practicing: Identifies tasks where AI could help. Experiments with AI workflows.
levels.capable: Redesigns workflows around AI capabilities rather than bolting AI onto existing processes.
levels.proficient: Sees AI-native solutions where others see traditional approaches. Rebuilds entire processes with AI.
levels.master: Defines new categories of work that didn't exist before AI. Others model their approach on this person.

data_structuring.label: Datafying the Un-datafied
data_structuring.description: Ability to turn qualitative, messy domains into structured, measurable ones

levels.aware: Recognizes that unstructured information has value waiting to be extracted.
levels.practicing: Structures qualitative data into categories. Tags and organizes information for retrieval.
levels.capable: Designs schemas for previously unstructured domains. Builds knowledge bases that are queryable.
levels.proficient: Creates measurement frameworks for domains others consider unmeasurable. The structured data reveals patterns no one saw.
levels.master: Turns an organization's implicit knowledge into structured, AI-augmentable systems.

rapid_ai_prototyping.label: Rapid AI Prototyping
rapid_ai_prototyping.description: Quickly tests AI ideas in practice to learn what works

levels.aware: Has experimented with no-code AI tools. Understands the concept of prompt iteration.
levels.practicing: Builds AI prototypes in days. Tests prompts systematically. Can demonstrate working AI workflows.
levels.capable: Ships AI MVPs in 2-3 weeks. Knows when to improve prompts vs. add knowledge vs. add MCP connections.
levels.proficient: Establishes rapid AI prototyping as team practice. Maintains a portfolio of tested AI solutions.
levels.master: Organization ships AI solutions faster than competitors because prototyping is cultural, not procedural.
